---
title: "Question Three"
format: 
  html: 
    theme: cyborg
---

Estimating the coverage of a percentile bootstrap CI 

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
library(foreach)
library(doParallel)
library(parallel)
```

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

B <- 1000
n <- 50
MC <- 2000
true_mean <- 1

cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1
cl <- makeCluster(cores)
registerDoParallel(cl)

boot_block <- function(M){

  out <- numeric(M)

  for(i in 1:M){

    x <- rexp(n, rate = 1)

    boot_samples <- matrix(sample(x, n * B, replace = TRUE), nrow = n)
    boot_means <- colMeans(boot_samples)

    CI <- quantile(boot_means, c(0.025, 0.975))

    out[i] <- (CI[1] <= true_mean & CI[2] >= true_mean)
  }

  out
}

# split work across cores
jobs <- rep(MC/cores, cores)

results <- foreach(j = jobs, .combine = c) %dopar% {
  boot_block(j)
}

stopCluster(cl)

coverage <- mean(results)
round(coverage,2 )
```
